methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Material Evolution, a Series A housing and construction company founded by Liz Gilligan and Sam Clark, has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by a limited comp universe: two private peers (Inversion Space and Cobod) are available, with no direct public comparables in the frontier construction technology space.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $15M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $30M. This multiplier reflects typical dilution and value creation patterns observed in Series A to subsequent-stage progressions within the sector. Given the limited comp depth and single methodological approach, triangulation is necessarily narrow.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $34M, with a median of $30M. The range reflects uncertainty around the appropriate step-up multiple and the absence of market-derived comp multiples on revenue, EBITDA, or other operational metrics. Key uncertainties include Material Evolution's current revenue run-rate and unit economics relative to Inversion Space and Cobod, which would anchor more granular relative valuation. The shallow data set also precludes assessment of sector-specific discount or premium factors. A more robust valuation would require operational metrics and fuller transparency on comparable company performance.
